meta.description: 42yo founder, time-starved, values brevity. Healthy baseline, normal labs, WHOOP-only wearable. Represents the modal user for the 'Time-Starved Achiever' archetype. Used as the baseline for happy-path evals.
meta.primaryStressTest: Baseline correctness — Jeffrey must be tight, on-brand, no over-explanation.
meta.fictionDisclaimer: Fictional. Any resemblance to a real person is coincidental.

lifestyleNotes: Light alcohol use (2-3 drinks per week)

wearables.summary: 90 days of synthesized WHOOP data. Avg HRV 58ms (range 45-72). Avg recovery 62%. Avg sleep 6h 30m weekdays. Notable: HRV declined 8% during weeks 6-8 (work travel period), recovered week 9.

adherence.summary: First 14 days: 92% adherence. Week 3: dropped to 71% (travel). Week 4: recovered to 88%. Last 7 days: 85%.

ingredients.name: Vitamin D3
ingredients.rationale: Baseline 32 ng/mL → low-normal. Target 40-60 ng/mL for energy and immune support.

ingredients.name: Omega-3 (EPA+DHA)
ingredients.rationale: Omega-3 index 5.2% (low). Target ≥8%. Supports cognitive performance — aligned with focus goal.

ingredients.name: Magnesium glycinate
ingredients.rationale: Sleep duration <7h on weekdays. Glycinate form for evening dosing without GI effect.

ingredients.name: Methylated B-complex
ingredients.rationale: Energy goal + travel cadence + alcohol use. B12 normal but supportive.

priorConversations.summary: Marcus mentioned he prefers evening dosing for anything that's not strictly morning-required because he often skips morning routines when traveling. Confirmed magnesium PM is fine. Asked about taking omega-3 at night — Jeffrey explained either is fine but with-meal preferred.

extractedFacts.content: Prefers PM dosing when possible due to travel disruption of AM routine

priorConversations.summary: Marcus reported feeling more focused since starting omega-3 4 weeks ago. Asked when he'd see lab confirmation. Jeffrey explained ~3 month timeline for omega-3 index to shift meaningfully.

extractedFacts.content: User reports subjective focus improvement on omega-3 protocol

memorySeed.content: Prefers concise responses. Skip caveats unless safety-critical.
Prefers PM dosing when travel disrupts AM routine
Reported subjective focus improvement on omega-3 protocol week 4
